description: A photograph depicting a straightened water course, likely a river or canal, flowing between wide, concrete-lined banks within an urban environment. On the left side, multi-story residential buildings are visible behind landscaped green areas and young trees. In the background, a distant city skyline is present under a clear blue sky. The water surface is calm, reflecting the sky. This image exemplifies a "highly modified water course and canal" (potentially CORINE Land Cover (CLC) or CLC+ class 8.1.2), characterized by significant man-made interventions such as concrete bedding and straightened river shores, as referenced in the accompanying text for differentiating land cover classes under guidelines like those for the Water Framework Directive.
